guide_metadata.topic: Ignition Interlock (IID) Financial Assistance & Community Resources
guide_metadata.legal_basis: Oregon Revised Statutes (ORS) 813.603 - Indigent Waiver Standards

section_1_iid_financial_waiver_process.overview: SNAP recipients automatically meet 'Indigence Standards' for a full or partial waiver of IID costs. A court order is typically required to force vendors to honor this.

steps.title: Obtain Affidavit of Indigency
steps.instructions: Go to Washington County Circuit Court. Ask the clerk for the 'Motion and Order for Waiver of IID Costs'.

steps.title: Secure Judge's Signature
steps.instructions: Fill out the motion at the courthouse. A judge must sign it. This order legally compels the vendor to waive fees.

steps.title: Contact Vendor
steps.instructions: Call the local number (not the national hotline if possible). State clearly: 'I have a court order for an indigent fee waiver and I am on SNAP.'

holiday_assistance.name: Tualatin Valley Fire & Rescue 'Toy & Joy'
holiday_assistance.action: Call for local partner agency referral.

holiday_assistance.name: Salvation Army Washington County
holiday_assistance.description: Angel Tree toys and holiday food boxes.

holiday_assistance.name: Santa's Posse
holiday_assistance.description: Food and toy delivery by local law enforcement.
holiday_assistance.action: Contact child's school counselor for referral.

food_and_groceries.name: Double Up Food Bucks
food_and_groceries.description: Matches SNAP spent on produce $1-for-$1 (up to $20/day).
food_and_groceries.location: Participating stores (e.g., WinCo) and Farmers Markets.

food_and_groceries.name: Urban Gleaners (Vose Elementary Market)
food_and_groceries.description: Free fresh food market, no ID required.

mother_and_child.name: Mother & Child Education Center
mother_and_child.description: Free diapers, wipes, baby clothes (0-5T), maternity wear.

mother_and_child.name: PDX Diaper Bank
mother_and_child.action: Must get referral from DHS Caseworker or WIC officer.

mother_and_child.name: Clothes for Kids
mother_and_child.description: Free school wardrobe for Beaverton School District students.

mother_and_child.name: WIC Washington County
mother_and_child.description: Nutrition for moms and kids under 5.

health_and_disability.name: Washington County DAVS
health_and_disability.description: Disability, Aging & Veteran Services - central hub for benefits counseling.

health_and_disability.name: Virginia Garcia Memorial Health Center
health_and_disability.description: Medical and dental care on sliding scale (often $0).

legal_and_housing.name: Legal Aid Services of Oregon (Hillsboro)
legal_and_housing.description: Free legal help for low-income clients.
legal_and_housing.address: 230 NE 2nd Ave, Suite F, Hillsboro, OR 97124

section_4_faq.question: Can the housing coordinator's 'Declaration of Homelessness' form be used for IID waivers?
section_4_faq.answer: No. While the eligibility criteria are similar, IID vendors and the Court require a specific 'Motion and Order for Waiver of IID Costs'.
section_4_faq.corrective_action: Use the housing form as proof of status to obtain the correct court order from the Washington County Circuit Court.
